architecture_fit.note: Single attention head whose query is a Hadamard (bilinear) interaction of the two operand embeddings — minimal delta from base_model.py's additive head — and it genuinely multiplies (1.0 routing).

baseline_comparison.note: Trained head (1.0) is shown against the additive a+b baseline (0.315), the hand-built circuit, and the ⊙→+ ablation all under the same harness.

faithfulness.note: Same trained weights with ⊙ swapped for + collapses routing to 0.14, below the additive baseline — a clean causal test that the multiplication does the routing.

operating_range.note: Holds at 1.0 routing accuracy and ~1.0 attended mass across the full K∈{2,4,8,16,32} sweep with no silent failure (robustness 1.0).

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: A genuine hand-built (d,d,d) bilinear tensor from dual operand bases reproduces the routing at 1.0 — mechanism by construction, not argmax-decode + lookup.

visual_judgement.note: Grouped per-K bar chart (trained/handbuilt/baseline/ablation + chance), attended-value-vs-product scatter, and training curve make the claim legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties the y-axis (routing accuracy), x-axis (operand range K), baselines, and ablation directly to the goal's headline metric and its hardest axis.

notes: An exemplary attempt: a single-head Hadamard-product query directly answers whether one head can multiply, reaching 1.0 routing accuracy and attended mass across the entire K sweep while the additive baseline decays from 0.56 to 0.155. The causal story is airtight — a same-weights ⊙→+ ablation collapses to below baseline, and a hand-built bilinear tensor reproduces the result by construction. Visualisation and rationale cleanly map onto the goal's headline metric, leaving nothing material to critique.
